name: High-Stakes Spoken Assessment Uses Synthetic Off-Topic Cases for Detection Rather Than Scorer Validation
statement: Wang et al. provide adjacent high-stakes assessment evidence for using synthetic or constructed response sets to train and evaluate validity-protection detectors, but the synthetic cases are mismatched real spoken responses used for off-topic detection rather than generated responses used to validate automated scores.
population_or_context: Large-scale high-stakes English speaking assessment for non-native speakers.
time_horizon: Full text retrieved and extracted on 2026-06-03.
quantitative_note: The best Inception-ResNet-v2 model achieved F1 = 92.8% on the off-topic class, compared with baseline F1 = 85.5%.
caveats: The synthetic off-topic cases are created by prompt-response mismatch rather than by LLM generation.
The target task is off-topic detection for downstream scoring protection, not automated score validation.
Authentic off-topic responses are rare, so generalization to real off-topic behavior remains bounded.
evaluation_method: Train/test classification of on-topic versus synthetic off-topic spoken responses, evaluated with F1 on the off-topic class.
limitations: Adjacent to synthetic student response scoring; included to clarify a high-stakes stress-testing/detection boundary.
Does not establish acceptable use of synthetic responses for final scoring, fairness validation, or deployment readiness.

source_locator.quote_or_paraphrase_note: The finding paraphrases the high-stakes speaking context, synthetic off-topic construction, train/test corpus, and F1 results.
